title: Boulder Co Vlog 4/6/18: Roller Rode 50 Mi 3Hrs, finished taxes

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his April 6th day: he slept poorly for the second night in a row, spent the day at his mom's house doing taxes (which cost around $360 through TurboTax), then came home and rode his bike on rollers for three hours, covering about 50 miles with a suffer score of 161. He reflects on his incremental training approach inspired by NASA's baby-steps philosophy and expresses excitement about his current solo training environment.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Believes in baby steps rather than jumping to maximum effort, inspired by NASA's methodical approach
Excited that living alone with no distractions is the ideal situation for serious training, unlike high school
Thinks ~$360 for TurboTax is expensive and not cheaper than H&R Block as he expected

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode my bike for 70 miles and got a suffer score of 161, which is extreme.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was up all night and didn't sleep at all the night before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I set my alarm for 5 a.m. but only slept until 3 a.m.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to my mom's house to do tax stuff, which took all day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode for three hours today, the longest I've ridden on rollers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been watching NASA videos and learning about the moon landing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm taking baby steps in my training to keep it comfortable.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I felt stronger during my ride today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm proud of my current state of 143 watts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to do a 20-minute power test soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to music while making videos at my mom's house.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided not to do my history tests as planned.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm recording my bike rides and watts to track progress.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm training in a way I've never tried before, focusing on consistency.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm excited about my current training routine because I have no distractions.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't been taking advantage of my time to ride, but now I can.
